Pricing Models:
Payments-as-a-Service vendors typically charge per transaction. The amount per transaction may change based on the number of transactions processed. Transactional charges vary based on the type of transaction. Charges are typically made for transactions such as those below. Vendors may have additional fees beyond those listed.
-Printing and mailing a paper check
-ACH fund transfer
ACH credit reversal
Issuing and initially funding a prepaid card
 Reloading a prepaid card
OFAC and death record search
Handling the 1099 process
Stop Payment
Void & Reissue
Printing and mailing a document (typically per page charge)
Escheated payment charge
Issuing and funding a virtual card
Multiparty payment (e.g. mortgagee payments or add 'l payees)
Total loss payments (these require additional signatures and processing)

module:
2. Payee Portal
Payee portal: A payee portal allows the payee to easily handle their payment processes online. Those intended for an individual usually allow the payee to set up their payment preference, add or manage accounts for payment deposits, and view real time status of payments. Businesses may have the additional capability of submitting their W-2 information, setting up bulk payments, and handling payment reconciliation online.

module:
4. Customer Service 
Payment-as-a-Service providers typically provide the frontline customer service to the payee, answering questions about the timing or amount of the payment, processing individual transactions such as stop payments, or providing support for changing payment preferences or accounts.

5. Administrative Support and Services
-Account Funding - Some vendors can use the insurers’ own bank accounts. Others set up a jointly held trust account where the payments are prefunded by the insurer until remitted to the payee.
-Escheatment - PaaS providers can handle the escheatment processes for each state as needed. They take on the payee notification and they turn over the funds to the state according to each state’s processes.
-Marketing Services - Most PaaS providers include some support for marketing. Some support insurer-branded cards. Some include assistance in developing marketing programs for you to drive demand for paper-free payments. Some support marketing messages within the payee portal.
-Reporting Tools
-Reporting Data - real time data or regular data files that aggregate data across all the payment channels for easy reconciliation.
-Reconciliation Tools - The process of matching up payment transactions in your accounting system becomes more complex when multiple payment methods are used.
-Administrative Portal - The portal provides the insurer with access to data and administrative tools. Typical features include:
• Access to scheduled reports or ad hoc reporting tools.
• Reconciliation tools and/or the ability to export reconciliation files.
• Administrative access to add insurer users and manage their permissions.
• Search by claim number, named insured, named payee, date of payment, and other dimensions.
• Manage and view real time status of each payment
• Tools to take specific actions on specific payments, such as stop payment or void/reissue.
• Tools that make it easy to add marketing and/or cross-selling messages on the portal.
-Payment Integrity

Core Collections Activities:

1. ANALYTICS LED RISK PRIORITIZATION & FORECASTING
Dynamic risk scoring based on industry level macro variables, historical account performance and external factors as applicable
Dynamic risk scoring based on industry level macro variables, historical account performance and external factors as applicable:
Automated prioritization of activities based on customer type, collection, risk, balance and location
Standardized dispute resolution with standard routing, escalation and approval processes
Data from banks, external remittance data, electronic data from customers, or any other supporting data used to improve auto-match% and better cash forecasting


2. AUTOMATED COMMUNICATIONS & CHASERS
Automated reminders pre due date based on inherent risk; Automated chasers post due date based on ageing

3. REAL TIME CASH APPLICATION
Real-time Cash Application integrated with collections, dispute resolution and self-service portal


4. COMPREHENSIVE DATA REPORTS AND DASHBOARDS
Stringent Credit Risk Management - automated credit reviews and customer score cards
Reporting engine – Collections Dashboard, AR aging report, On Account and Misapplied Cash

Other Receivables Areas:

1. Analytics driven Post Billing activities: Run AR reports from the system (by product / broker / location) and run Analytics engines to determine likelihood of default for more targeted focus. Enhance outreach and connect with Insured / broker by confirm in invoice receipt by the right contact at the client / broker and share any information required. 

2. Intelligent pro-active dispute resolution and automated reminder Pre-Due: Identify cases with high propensity for disputes and escalate and help resolve disputes (revisions/credit/other). Automated reminders at various stages and confirmation of the scheduled payment date (Promise to Pay)

3. Intelligent Reporting and Tracking: Consistently and proactively move the invoice to the payments stages,  effectively collect and proactively escalate, send Overdue statement if payment not received as per “Promise to Pay”, loop in brokers and account managers (as needed) in collections email

4. Dispute Resolution: Create and follow dynamic follow escalation matrix to resolve the dispute with client / broker, create processes for quick and efficient dispute resolution, Intelligent solution to capture  resolutions and post resolution, confirm schedule payment date on disputed invoices

5. Reporting - Create Single Versions of Truth: Optimize system reports as “version of truth” for all collections reporting and follow-up; Report, track and benchmark DSO, Overdue AR, disputes, bad-debts and other KPI’s; Quantify the $’s to be collected on a time scale

6. Additional Value Add: Create customized ‘risk’ segments leveraging multiple criterion like balance/tenure, likelihood-to-pay, tenure, time to payment  and other models. Assign these collections risk scores and portfolio risk ranking to prioritize collections and minimize loss. Proactive feedback loop for credit team to better manage credit risk. Leverage customer intel across product lines (behavior patterns, terms, contracts, shutouts, etc.) to give clients better understanding of customer shifts
outcomes:
Better Cash Management - Focused Collections and Customer Service Effort on cases with very high probability of default to improve DSOs

Improved speed of dispute resolution & billing rectification - better customer outcomes

Better staffing & planning of Receivables Management operations

Better capture of customer behavior & industry behavior data at billing / dispute and collections stage for better front end deaccessioning

Improved UW Deaccessioning

module:
As part of this offering EXL proposes to perform a health-check of business processes to identify high risk areas, assess new & increased risks, and develop actions to address business critical priorities.

'COE based delivery structure:
- Process, risk, controls and organizational taxonomy
- Process flows, risks, controls
- Risk and Controls Matrix
- Review testing methodology and guidelines 
- Develop Test Scripts for key controls
- Perform test-of-one 
- Evaluate design effectiveness of key control(s)
- Report on the status of controls tested, its effectiveness and open gaps 

Key levers to be used for desired outcome are as follows:
Automation / Robotics : Advanced automation and robotics interventions directed at select SOX  compliance tasks and underlying processes 

Analytics & Reporting : Insightful and granular analysis of data to identify high risk areas and detect potential exceptions early 

Leading Practices : Implement risk based testing methodologies and work-paper documentation standards for increased external audit reliance 

Dual-Shore : Large pool of SOX auditors, data scientists and automation SMEs both onshore and offshore
outcomes:
~35-40% reduction in cost of compliance 

Increased assurance through automated testing of entire population as opposed to sample testing

Early identification of deficiencies for remediation prior to year end 

Standardized controls testing documentation resulting in increased external audit reliance 

Reduced manual effort – automation of select SOX compliance activities and underlying processes
